vocabulary.definition: The sum of all values divided by the number of values (average)
The middle value when data is ordered from least to greatest
A value that is much greater or less than most other values in a data set
vocabulary.visual: Data: 12, 14, 13, 15, 45 → 45 is far from the cluster, so it is an outlier

vocabulary.definition: When data bunches toward one end with a tail stretching to the other
vocabulary.visual: Scores: 5, 6, 7, 8, 8, 35 → most scores are low, but 35 creates a tail to the right (skewed right)

vocabulary.definition: The shape and spread of a data set
vocabulary.visual: Symmetric = even on both sides. Skewed = bunched on one side with a tail

vocabulary.definition: How spread out the values in a data set are

launch.narrative: The league is creating awards for the season. For the scoring title, they need to pick the best measure of a typical game. Here are the top scorer's points per game: 22, 24, 20, 25, 23, 21, 58. That 58-point game was a record-breaker! Should the league report the mean (27.6) or the median (23) as the player's typical scoring?
launch.contextImage: Trophy display case with player stats and a giant 58 highlighted
noticePrompts: How does the 58-point game compare to the other scores?
What is the mean? What is the median?
Which measure is closer to what this player usually scores?
wonderPrompts: When does an outlier make the mean misleading?
Is there a rule for when to use mean vs. median?

explore.label: Sort each sports data scenario into the correct category: Use Mean or Use Median.

cards.text: Basketball: Points per game — 18, 20, 19, 22, 21, 20 (no outliers, symmetric)

discourse.prompt: What pattern do you notice about when median is the better choice?
discourse.sentenceFrame: The median is better when the data has ___ because the mean gets pulled toward ___. The mean is better when the data is ___ because ___.

optional.stem: A data set has an extreme outlier. Which measure of center is usually best?
optional.explanation: The median is resistant to outliers, so it better represents typical values.

optional.stem: Which measure of spread pairs naturally with the mean?
optional.explanation: MAD measures spread around the mean.

optional.stem: Test scores are 80, 82, 85, 88, 12. Why is the median better than the mean here?
choices: The score of 12 is an outlier that pulls the mean down
optional.explanation: The outlier 12 lowers the mean a lot, but the median stays near the typical scores.

optional.stem: For data with no outliers and a symmetric shape, which center is appropriate?
optional.explanation: With no outliers, the mean represents the data well.

approaching.stem: A gymnast's scores are: 8.5, 8.8, 8.7, 8.6, 8.9. There are no outliers. Which measure best represents a typical score?
choices: Neither — the data is too small
approaching.explanation: The data is symmetric with no outliers, so the mean (8.7) best represents the typical score.

approaching.stem: A runner's mile times are: 7:10, 7:15, 7:12, 7:20, 12:00. The 12:00 was due to a cramp. Which measure best represents a typical mile?
choices: Median — the outlier 12:00 pulls the mean too high
Mean — it uses all the data
Mode — it shows the most common time
approaching.explanation: The 12:00 is an outlier that pulls the mean up. The median (7:15) better represents the runner's typical time.

approaching.stem: Match each data description with the best measure of center.
Data set: 5, 6, 7, 7, 8, 50. The mean is about 13.8 and the median is 7. Which better represents the typical value?
choices: Median (7) — the outlier 50 inflates the mean
approaching.explanation: The mean (13.8) is higher than 5 of the 6 values because the outlier 50 pulls it up. The median (7) better represents a typical value.

approaching.instructions: Sort each data feature into whether it suggests using the mean or the median.

categories.label: Use Median
items: Data has one or more outliers
Data is skewed to one side
One extreme value pulls the average

hints: The mean is sensitive to extreme values — outliers pull it up or down.
The median stays in the middle regardless of how extreme the highest or lowest values are.
approaching.label: Sort each feature by which measure of center it supports.

onLevel.stem: A baseball player's batting averages over 6 seasons are: .280, .295, .290, .285, .300, .110. The .110 was an injury-shortened season. Which measure better represents the player's typical batting average?
choices: Median, because the .110 outlier pulls the mean down
Mean, because it uses all the data
Mean, because .110 is a real season
onLevel.explanation: The .110 is an outlier that pulls the mean down to .260. The median (.2875) better represents typical performance because it isn't affected by the extreme value.

editableCells.answer: Median (72 pulls the mean to 27.6, higher than 4 of 5 values)

onLevel.stem: Match the reason with whether to use mean or median.

pairs.left: Data is symmetric and has no extreme values
Data has one or more outliers
Data is skewed to one side
All values are clustered close together

onLevel.label: Data: 10, 12, 11, 13, 50. Place the mean (19.2) and median (12) on the number line. Notice how far apart they are because of the outlier.
hints: The mean (19.2) is pulled toward the outlier 50. The median (12) stays near the cluster of data.
When the mean and median are far apart, that is a sign of skewed data or an outlier.

extending.title: Find the Reasoning Error

workedExample.label: Conclusion
workedExample.work: The mean (28.2) is the best measure because it uses all the data.

extending.correctWork: The median (32.5) is the better measure. The value 5 is an outlier (the player was injured that game) that pulls the mean down to 28.2, which is lower than any of the player's healthy games. The median (32.5) better represents typical minutes played.
hints: Look at the value 5. Does it fit with the rest of the data?
The mean (28.2) is lower than 5 of the 6 values. Does that seem like a 'typical' game?

extending.prompt: A local newspaper reports that the average home price in a neighborhood is $450,000. The actual prices are: $180K, $200K, $190K, $210K, $195K, $1,725K. Is the reporter's claim misleading? Calculate the mean and median and explain which better represents a 'typical' home price.
extending.sentenceFrame: Mean = ___. Median = ___. The reporter's claim is ___ because the outlier ($1,725K) pulls the mean ___. The ___ (about $___) better represents a typical home price because ___.

extending.instructions: Sort each real-world situation into whether the mean or median is more commonly reported.

categories.label: Mean is typically reported
items: Average test score for a class (no outliers)

categories.label: Median is typically reported
items: Median household income in a city
Median home price in a neighborhood

hints: Income and home prices often have extreme outliers (billionaires, mansions), so median is used.
Temperature and test scores are often symmetric, so mean works well.
extending.label: Sort by which measure is typically used in real life.

connect.scenario: A sports reporter writes: 'The average ticket price for the playoffs is $85.' The actual prices are: $45, $50, $48, $52, $55, $260. The $260 ticket is a courtside seat.
connect.promptQuestion: Is $85 a fair way to describe a 'typical' ticket price? What measure should the reporter use?
connect.prompt: The reporter used the ___, which is ___. A better measure would be the ___ (about ___) because ___. The courtside seat at $260 is an ___ that pulls the mean ___.

exitTicket.stem: Data set: 15, 18, 16, 17, 15, 72. Which measure of center best represents the data?
choices: Mean, because it uses all values
Mean, because it is always best
exitTicket.explanation: The value 72 is an outlier. The mean (25.5) is pulled high by 72 and doesn't represent the typical values. The median (16.5) is a better measure of center.
